name: S-56
events: 10 Apr 1943 S-56 fires two torpedoes against ' what is identified as ' a merchant of 8000 tons off the Tanafjord. Both torpedoes missed their target.

14 Apr 1943 S-56 fires four torpedoes against ' what is identified as ' a merchant of 6000 tons off the Kongsfjord. All four torpedoes missed their target. The German merchant Detlef (1809 GRT) reported being missed by 10 meters.

17 May 1943 S-56 attacked a German convoy and torpedoed and sank the German tanker Eurostadt (1118 GRT) off the Kongsfjord in position 70°48'N, 29°34'E. The German merchant Wartheland (3676 GRT, former Soviet Arija) is hit by a dud torpedo during the same attack and was slightly damaged.
17 Jul 1943 S-56 torpedoed and sank the German minesweeper M 346 (551 tons) west of the Tanafjord in position 71°07'N, 28°22'E. S-56 misses the German auxiliary minelayer Ostmark in the same attack.
19 Jul 1943 S-56 torpedoed and sank the German auxiliary patrol vessel NKi 09 / Alane (466 GRT, former British ASW trawler HMS Warwickshire) off the Tanafjord near Gamvik.
20 Jan 1944 S-56 attacked a German convoy off the Tanafjord but fails to hit any ships.
23 Jan 1944 S-56 tries to attack a German convoy off Nordkyn. S-56 attacked on the surface but was spotted and driven off by gunfire from the German escorts NKi 10 and UJ 1206.
31 Jan 1944 S-56 torpedoed and sank the German merchant Heinrich Schulte (5056 GRT) west of the Tanafjord in position 71°08'N, 28°17'E.

4 Mar 1944 S-56 attacked a German convoy of the Tanafjord in position 71°04'N, 28°34'E. No hits were obtained and one torpedo failed to leave it's tube. The attack was made at 25 meters depth on sonar.
15 Jul 1944 S-56 attacked a German convoy off the Syltefjord in position 70°38'N, 30°36'E. S-56 fires four torpedoes but no hits were obtained.
24 Sep 1944 S-56 attacked a German convoy off Nordkyn with four torpedoes. No hits were obtained
26 Sep 1944 S-56 attacked the German minesweepers M 31 and M 251 off Nordkyn. No hits were obtained and S-56 was damaged during a counter-attack.
